channel.title: Deep South Ventures
channel.description: Start with a great domain name

item.title: Traction then taste
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
You’re gonna have to build lots of things to find one thing (that gets traction). Then, when things settle, you get to take a big-ass bite of the metaphorical sandwich you built. You took a few nibbles early on, but decided to overlook the unusual flavor. CHoMP oh, you still don’t like it? it’s actually ... Read more

The post Traction then taste appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Clearing the cache of my life
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
Command-Q; force quit; emptiness. I recently got a new computer and it presented an option to port over my current installation. Everything. My self-righteous mind instructed me that everything I’ve installed & saved is quite significant and critical for continued, uninterrupted digital affairs. I found myself – immediately – questioning this unconscious answer. Why. Why ... Read more

The post Clearing the cache of my life appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: I have no goals, yet everything is accomplished
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
But, but, Peter, you need some north star, something to aim for… to challenge yourself… a goal ! No I don’t. A bird doesn’t need a goal when building a nest. They, simply, begin building. Trees don’t wake up one morning and pronounce: “My plan this year is to grow an additional 3 feet“. They ... Read more

The post I have no goals, yet everything is accomplished appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Inflection points & used carpet padding
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
When I discovered my talent as a grunt worker: After my first layoff, I wandered over to my brother’s carpet business and began to hang out (I had nothing better to do). This was around 1997. I’d see things laying around, and offer to clean them up or organize them. There’d be an 8ft pile ... Read more

The post Inflection points & used carpet padding appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: On being laid off & unplanned entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
〰️ it’s weird to look back; I sometimes get confused on how I got here 〰️ Most folks dream of being an entrepreneur; “a path that seemed inevitable“, they say. None of that shit applies to me. I’m only here cause I kept getting laid off and that nonsense infuriated me. I treated my first ... Read more

The post On being laid off & unplanned entrepreneurship appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Now. That’s what’s next
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
“So what’s next for you?” It’s a question I’m sometimes asked, and I often feel obligated to blurt out some random ‘project’ or half baked idea I’d been chewing on. But lately, the answer has never felt sincere, mainly because I usually have nothing in the hopper. I was simply focusing on current projects. Then ... Read more

The post Now. That’s what’s next appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: The homesteading hippie vagabond
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
He lives inside me. This misfit; the homesteading hippie vagabond. He’s set-up shop in there, and seems quite comfortable. Being a builder, his presence can be quite a challenge. I try to sway him toward revenue-focused building efforts & “rocket-ship” growth opportunities, but he shrugs. His hippie nature prefers alternatives. Somehow, over the years, he ... Read more

The post The homesteading hippie vagabond appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: The dude that built DudeRanch.com
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
[orig published in 2019; re-published & updated in 2023 & 2025] The outdoor patio was filled with dude ranchers. It was their yearly conference at Tanque Verde Ranch in Tucson, and I was there as a vendor (and first-time attendee). I remember saguaro cactus lining the landscape. It was early evening, the sun was setting, ... Read more

The post The dude that built DudeRanch.com appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: I don’t spin plates; I manage hot tubs
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
them : “So tell me a little about what you do …” me : “uh, I’m a domain investor, but tend to build on domains I acquire (vs flip, etc). I have 3 or 4 projects I oversee.” them : “3 or 4 projects!? … sounds like a lot of spinning plates …” me : ... Read more

The post I don’t spin plates; I manage hot tubs appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Just an ounce of encouragement
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
… that’s all I needed. It’s what my brother gave me. I’m not sure if I would have walked down this path had he not. The idea I pitched him was ludicrous – to purchase & install a high resolution camera at college football stadiums, pointed at certain sections. When exciting plays occurred, snap a ... Read more

The post Just an ounce of encouragement appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: My life is a litmus test
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
I’m never quite sure what pursuits might strike my fancy; so I’ll try most anything & study it like a litmus test. Toss that shit into a petry dish, stir it up, & dip my awareness into it.  what     color     did     that     thing     turn?  I won’t know until I try. ... Read more

The post My life is a litmus test appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: How on earth I became an entrepreneur
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
The email landed in my inbox around 10:05/am, alerting us of an all-hands meeting in 15 minutes. “Guess we got acquired”, we all thought. 16 minutes later, we were all laid off. This was February of 2001. I was first alerted of my uselessness in 1995, when ~ fresh out of college ~ I worked ... Read more

The post How on earth I became an entrepreneur appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Going up the hill
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
I should figure out whether this is a metaphor, simile, or analogy. There’s a walking path near my house, frequented by most Atlanta residents. Right alongside the Chattahoochee River. Lots of foot traffic, lots of neat outdoor scenery – all within city limits. There’s one section of the path where foot traffic dies down. It’s ... Read more

The post Going up the hill appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: part two, I sell onions on the Internet
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
** looks at watch ** oh my Lordy … it’s been two years. If you remember, I sell onions on the internet. Vidalia onions, to be exact.. You can read part one here if you want. The thought of following up that first post never occurred to me, until it did. And then it sorta ... Read more

The post part two, I sell onions on the Internet appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Building from a place of surrender
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
I was caught in this trap for years. While I felt I’d assembled the online experience & background to build, I felt the need to continue asking questions. Sahil is right : 👇 👇 👇 And here I was: “What backend are you using?” “Should I build on PHP or Ruby on Rails?” “Is this ... Read more

The post Building from a place of surrender appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Behind the scenes of a carpet pad recycling business in 1997
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
Who are you again? That’s right – apologies – our new hire. Welcome aboard. Let me get you up to speed on how things operate around here. Do you like things that smell? Don’t answer that, ’cause it doesn’t matter. Our focus is smelly shit. You ever seen that padding underneath carpeting? That’s the smelly ... Read more

The post Behind the scenes of a carpet pad recycling business in 1997 appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Building things [ that do just one thing ]
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
There’s no need to make this complicated. People want to make it complicated. My brain isn’t that smart – I need to dumb things down. And it ends up, dumbing things down can usually elevate your chances of success. When I consider a project, I like to compare it to a washing machine. Washing machines ... Read more

The post Building things [ that do just one thing ] appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Let go, then go
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
Loosen your grip; just a little will help. You can release it all if you want. I promise you won’t notice once you leave it behind, cause you’re moving forward. It’s so freeing, to disrobe, and discard that identity. and start again. We’re allowed to do that. Take a new first step. You don’t even ... Read more

The post Let go, then go appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Must ride mule (to & from) work location
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
“David, this one, tell me about it …” He’d let me look at his domain name portfolio. Not names he owned and parked, but domains he owned & developed. This was another early project of his – a job board solely focused on the ranching industry. All aspects – cattle, outfitters, dude/guest, hunting, and even ... Read more

The post Must ride mule (to & from) work location appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Benevolent, uplifting cuss words
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
This website inspired me yesterday. We need more sites like this. Direct, blunt. No apologies. And lots of cuss words. There aren’t many places online where you can soak in free-flowing, un-edited, casual-speech cursing. Not anger based. Just honest, wholesome profanity. Creative profanity. Uplifting profanity. Positive profanity. Saying shit like this. Or reflecting on something ... Read more

The post Benevolent, uplifting cuss words appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Give me grunt work
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
It’s slowly become my secret weapon; my strongest talent. The binary tasks // The cut-and-paste projects // The mundane metrics collection. 💩 work The dregs. It may sound strange to admit, but I enjoy this type of endeavor. As a solo builder, I feel it’s necessary. Not easy, but necessary … and [oddly] satisfying. Peter ... Read more

The post Give me grunt work appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: I sell onions on the Internet
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
Vidalia Onions to be exact. They’re classified as a sweet onion, and because of their mild flavor (they don’t make your eyes tear up), some folks can eat them like an apple. Most of my customers do. During a phone order one season – 2018 I believe – a customer shared this story where he ... Read more

The post I sell onions on the Internet appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Slow down & wander
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.description:
Wanna know what I did a few days back? I spoke on the phone with a one-off customer for 40 minutes. Just talking. A meandering conversation on our love of the South. Just because. It felt good. Because I could. Because I’m not under pressure by a heavy handed VC to achieve a 30x revenue ... Read more

The post Slow down & wander appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Window shopping for expired Domain Names
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.category: Build a Side Business on the Internet
item.description:
[ 🆕 updated Sept 2023; added GName.com domain backordering ] aka. Part 2 – Want to Build a Side Business? Just Buy a Great Domain Name Previously, we suggested the idea of acquiring an expired domain name as the impetus for building a side business. No idea required. The domain will be your inspiration. And ... Read more

The post Window shopping for expired Domain Names appeared first on Deep South Ventures.

item.title: Want to build a side business? Just buy a great Domain Name
item.dc:creator: Peter Askew
item.category: Build a Side Business on the Internet
item.description:
[updated April, 2022] So you wanna start a side business while holding down a full-time job. Kool. Dang smart move – that’s the same route I took – ten years ago now. Self-funded, no VC to appease, no angel investor to update. Just you, and your sandbox of ambition. Hmm, but where to start? Which ... Read more

The post Want to build a side business? Just buy a great Domain Name appeared first on Deep South Ventures.
